name: Deviled Smelt
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse smelt under cold running water and pat dry on paper towels.
2. In a plastic bag, combine flour, salt, mustard, cayenne pepper, paprika, and lemon peel. Add smelt and shake well until fish are evenly coated.
3. Heat oil in a deep-fat fryer or saucepan to 375°F (190°C) or until a small cube of day-old bread browns in 40 seconds.
4. Place half of the smelt in a frying basket and lower it gradually into hot oil. Fry for 1 minute, shaking the basket frequently. Drain on paper towels.
5. Reheat oil to 375°F (190°C) and repeat with remaining smelt.
6. Place all smelt in the basket and fry for an additional 1-2 minutes or until lightly golden and crisp. Drain on paper towels.
7. Turn fish into a warm serving dish, sprinkle with chopped parsley, and serve hot with lemon wedges. Garnish with lemon peel strips and dill sprigs, if desired.
